ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for melon pudding with nata de coco; closest parent 'pudding food product' (FOODON_03301710) used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKP (Puddings and similar desserts) is the closest parent category; no specific code for melon pudding with nata de coco exists in the FoodEx2 catalogue.

main_ingredients: nata de coco (bacterial cellulose coconut gel)

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is one pre-packaged cup of approximately 100 g, as sold by Naspac and served at The Noodle Place, Circular Road, Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Gelatin dessert (jelly/pudding) with fruit, ready-to-eat; adapted with nata de coco composition from USDA Branded Food entries for coconut gel (nata de coco)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA entry exists for 'Melon Pudding with Nata de Coco' as a packaged product. Values are estimated by combining USDA FoodData Central data for ready-to-eat gelatin/jelly dessert (FDC ID 169640, ~60 kcal/100g) with the low-calorie, high-fibre profile of nata de coco (bacterial cellulose gel, ~17 kcal/100g, ~0.5g dietary fibre per 100g per USDA Branded entries). The blended estimate reflects a typical 70:30 pudding-to-nata ratio. Naspac product label data is not publicly available; values should be verified against the physical product label when accessible.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC; gelatin/jelly dessert analogous estimate; University of Sydney GI Database entry for jelly/gelatin dessert
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Naspac Melon Pudding with Nata de Coco. GI estimated at 46 based on the University of Sydney GI Database entry for jelly/gelatin dessert (GI ~46, low category). Nata de coco (bacterial cellulose) contributes negligible glycaemic carbohydrate and does not raise the GI. Glycemic load per 100g serving = (46 × 13.0) / 100 = 5.98, rounded to 6.0. gi_category is 'low' per the ≤55 threshold rule.

hypertension.guidance: With approximately 25 mg sodium per 100g, this dessert poses minimal sodium load and is generally suitable for individuals managing hypertension when consumed in single-serve portions.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate free sugar content (~11.5g per 100g) despite low GI
type2_diabetes.guidance: Although the estimated GI is low (~46), the free sugar content of approximately 11.5g per 100g serving warrants portion control for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes; consumption should be factored into the daily carbohydrate budget and monitored for postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Very low fat and saturated fat content
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: With negligible total fat (<0.2g per 100g) and effectively zero saturated fat, this dessert does not contribute meaningfully to cardiovascular lipid risk, though its added sugar content should be considered within an overall heart-healthy dietary pattern.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Melon Pudding with Nata de Coco is a chilled, pre-packaged dessert product sold in Singapore, produced by Naspac and served at casual dining outlets such as The Noodle Place. The dessert consists of a lightly sweetened, melon-flavoured jelly or pudding base — typically set with agar or a similar gelling agent — embedded with translucent cubes of nata de coco, a chewy bacterial cellulose gel derived from fermented coconut water. The texture is soft and wobbly with intermittent chewy bites from the nata de coco pieces. The flavour profile is mildly sweet with a fresh, fruity melon note. Per 100g, the product provides approximately 55 kcal, 13g carbohydrate (of which ~11.5g is sugar), 0.5g protein, and 0.1g fat, making it a low-calorie, low-fat dessert option. The glycaemic index is estimated at approximately 46 (low GI), though the moderate free sugar content means individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should exercise portion awareness. Sodium is low (~25 mg per 100g), making it broadly suitable for those managing hypertension. The dessert is gluten-free and dairy-free. It is widely popular in Singapore as an affordable, refreshing dessert, particularly in warm weather, and is commonly found in hawker-adjacent casual dining settings. The product is priced at approximately SGD 1.90 per 100g cup.

confidence_reason: No exact nutritional entry for Naspac Melon Pudding with Nata de Coco was found in HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA FoodData Central, or any other approved primary source. Nutrient values are estimated by combining USDA FoodData Central data for ready-to-eat gelatin/jelly dessert with the known composition of nata de coco. GI is estimated from the closest analogous food (jelly/gelatin dessert) in the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is low until the physical Naspac product label or an HPB-registered entry is verified.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central analogous entries (gelatin/jelly dessert + nata de coco) and should be verified against the Naspac product label when accessible. 2. GI is an analogous estimate based on jelly/gelatin dessert (GI ~46) from the University of Sydney GI Database; nata de coco contributes negligible glycaemic carbohydrate. 3. saturated_fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero by formulation analogy; this dessert contains no appreciable fat source. monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g are 0.0 — confirmed zero by formulation analogy. trans_fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero by formulation analogy. cholesterol_mg is 0.0 — confirmed zero; no animal-derived fat ingredients. 4. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1 as it is not reported in the analogous source entries used. vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the analogous source entries used. vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the analogous source entries used. vitamin_b3_niacin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the analogous source entries used. vitamin_b6_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the analogous source entries used. vitamin_b12_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the analogous source entries used. folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not reported in the analogous source entries used. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the analogous source entries used. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the analogous source entries used. phosphorus_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the analogous source entries used. magnesium_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the analogous source entries used. zinc_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the analogous source entries used. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the analogous source entries used. 5. Vegan flag is false pending confirmation of gelling agent type (gelatin vs agar); if agar-based, the product would be vegan. 6. The cuisine field is set to 'Singaporean' as this is a Singapore-market packaged dessert product; the restaurant tag 'The Noodle Place - Circular Road' is preserved in the source metadata. 7. Price SGD 1.90 per 100g cup as recorded in field survey data.
